entities.name: HOUSEHOLD RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of farm households in drylands to withstand and recover from shocks to their livelihoods

entities.name: LIVELIHOOD HETEROGENEITY
entities.description: The diversity in livelihood strategies among households and its influence on their resilience

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS OF INDIA
entities.description: The geographical region in India where the study on household resilience was conducted

entities.name: RESILIENCE CAPACITY INDEX
entities.description: A metric devised based on food and non-food expenditure, cash savings, and food and feed reserves to quantify resilience

entities.name: MULTIVARIATE ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to identify livelihood types based on socioeconomic and biophysical variables

entities.name: HOUSEHOLD ASSETS
entities.description: The resources owned by a household, the lack of which is a primary cause of low resilience

entities.name: IRRIGATION
entities.description: Access to controlled water supply for crops, which strengthens household resilience

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Domesticated animals kept by households that contribute to strengthening resilience

entities.name: CROP DIVERSIFICATION
entities.description: The practice of growing multiple crop types to reduce risk and enhance household resilience

entities.name: RAINFED
entities.description: A farming condition dependent on rainfall, associated with lower resilience capacity

entities.name: TECHNOLOGY INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Targeted development actions aimed at improving household resilience
